task_completion.description: Verify that the agent initiated a crawl of the target website.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screaming Frog SEO Spider UI is open with 'https://crawler-test.com/' in the URL bar at the top, and the crawl progress bar shows activity or 100% completion.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent accessed the security headers data in the software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screaming Frog UI shows the 'Security' tab selected in the right-hand window or main view, displaying columns or filters for 'X-Frame-Options', 'X-Content-Type-Options', and 'Strict-Transport-Security'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the security audit data was exported to the correct CSV file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog or file manager/terminal showing 'security_audit.csv' being saved to or existing in the '/home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify that the summary report text file was created in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or file manager/terminal showing 'security_headers_summary.txt' being edited or existing in the '/home/ga/Documents/SEO/reports/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify that the summary report includes the total number of URLs crawled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'security_headers_summary.txt' are visible and explicitly state the total number of URLs crawled.

task_completion.description: Verify that the summary report includes the counts of URLs missing specific security headers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'security_headers_summary.txt' include specific numerical counts for URLs missing 'X-Frame-Options' and URLs missing 'X-Content-Type-Options'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the summary report includes a statement on HSTS enforcement.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'security_headers_summary.txt' include a clear 'Yes' or 'No' statement regarding whether HSTS (Strict-Transport-Security) is enforced.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used Screaming Frog SEO Spider to perform the audit.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show interaction with the Screaming Frog SEO Spider GUI to crawl the site and view security headers, rather than using terminal commands like curl or python scripts to bypass the software.

integrity.description: Verify that the CSV export was generated by the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should use the 'Export' button or 'Save' functionality within Screaming Frog to generate the CSV, rather than manually creating a mock CSV file in a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify that the summary report contains actual data derived from the crawl.
integrity.visual_evidence: The summary report should contain actual numbers (e.g., '15', '42') and a definitive 'Yes' or 'No' for HSTS, rather than placeholder text like '[Insert Count Here]' or 'X'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target URL is https://crawler-test.com/. The task requires using Screaming Frog SEO Spider v23.2. Expected export file is security_audit.csv in /home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports/. Expected report file is security_headers_summary.txt in /home/ga/Documents/SEO/reports/. The required headers to audit are X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, and Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS).
